scope.summary: Implement affine panel transform operations (scale_panel, rotate_panel, translate_panel) on the GarmentSpec Panel geometry as CRDT-safe edits, preserving cm units and grain direction through the transform
acceptance_criteria: scale_panel(panel_id, sx: f32, sy: f32, pivot_cm: [f32;2]) scales all EdgeShape vertices relative to the pivot; seam attachment points scale with the panel
rotate_panel(panel_id, angle_rad: f32, pivot_cm: [f32;2]) rotates all EdgeShape vertices; grain direction rotates by the same angle
translate_panel(panel_id, offset_cm: [f32;2]) moves all vertices; seam endpoints remain linked
All three emit TailorPanelCrdtUpdateRecorded; PANEL_CLOSED and SEAM_LENGTH_MATCH re-evaluated after transform
Model-accessible via edit_panel MCP tool with operation=transform
Uniform scale preserves grain-direction alignment with fabric grain metadata
scope.risk_if_missed: Without panel transforms, a model or operator cannot resize or reorient a panel produced by the primitive constructor or sketch-to-pattern without rewriting the entire coordinate array. Pattern symmetry (MT-125) and parametric sizing (MT-130) depend on consistent panel geometry; without a transform operation these produce mis-scaled results.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
